root_cause.explanation: Avatar imageUrl values in avatarManifest.json are hardcoded as http:// URLs (e.g. http://wp10665333.server-he.de/avatars/{id}.png). When the app is served over HTTPS (GitHub Pages production, all mobile browsers), browsers silently block these mixed-content HTTP image requests, causing all avatar images to fail loading with no visible error.
sequence: generate-avatar-manifest.mjs bakes absolute HTTP URLs into imageUrl fields at manifest generation time
avatarManifest.json is committed with 649 entries all using http://wp10665333.server-he.de/avatars/{id}.png
App is served over HTTPS on GitHub Pages; mobile browsers always enforce HTTPS
Browser mixed-content policy silently blocks HTTP image loads from HTTPS page
Image components render placeholders because src URL never resolves

evidence.description: endpointBaseUrl: 'http://wp10665333.server-he.de' — hardcoded HTTP protocol in runtime config
const BASE_URL = 'http://wp10665333.server-he.de' — HTTP base URL used at manifest generation time
imageUrl: `${BASE_URL}${relativeUrl}` — absolute HTTP URL baked into each manifest item
"imageUrl": "http://wp10665333.server-he.de/avatars/01000f37-a015-4997-8923-43b40c9ee182.png" — confirmed HTTP URLs in committed manifest data (all 649 entries)
src={avatar.imageUrl} — no crossOrigin, no onError fallback; silently shows placeholder on block
src={profile.avatar.imageUrl} — same pattern, no error handling
src={profile.avatar.imageUrl} — same pattern, no error handling

changes.change: Change BASE_URL from 'http://wp10665333.server-he.de' to '//wp10665333.server-he.de' (protocol-relative) so generated imageUrl values use protocol-relative URLs that inherit http or https from the page context
changes.reason: Removes the hardcoded http:// from the source of truth for manifest generation. Protocol-relative URLs let the browser choose the same protocol as the hosting page, eliminating mixed-content blocking.
This runtime config is used for any dynamic avatar URL construction. Must match the manifest fix so all avatar URL sources are consistent.

changes.change: Replace all 649 occurrences of '"imageUrl": "http://wp10665333.server-he.de/' with '"imageUrl": "//wp10665333.server-he.de/' using a global string replacement
changes.reason: The committed manifest is the actual data consumed at runtime. Without updating it, existing imageUrl values in the JSON remain http:// regardless of script changes. This is a data-layer fix to match the script fix.

changes.change: Keep fetch calls using BASE_URL with explicit https:// for server-side fetching (Node.js at manifest generation time does not have mixed-content restrictions). Only the stored imageUrl value should be protocol-relative.
changes.reason: Node.js build script needs an absolute URL with scheme to fetch from the server. Split into FETCH_BASE_URL (https://) for requests and STORED_BASE_URL (//) for the imageUrl field stored in the manifest.

invariants.api_contracts: AvatarItem.imageUrl type remains string; no interface changes
invariants.existing_behavior: All avatar filtering, selection, display, and persistence logic unchanged. Protocol-relative URLs resolve identically to http:// on HTTP and to https:// on HTTPS.
invariants.performance: No change — same CDN, same images, same lazy loading
invariants.backward_compatibility: Profiles with saved avatar.imageUrl containing http:// will still resolve (browsers follow the stored URL as-is). Only newly generated manifest and new selections get protocol-relative URLs.

fix_approach: In generate-avatar-manifest.mjs: introduce two constants — FETCH_BASE_URL = 'https://wp10665333.server-he.de' (for Node.js fetch calls) and STORED_BASE_URL = '//wp10665333.server-he.de' (for imageUrl values written to the manifest). Update imageUrl construction on line 119 to use STORED_BASE_URL. In profile.ts: change endpointBaseUrl to '//wp10665333.server-he.de'. In avatarManifest.json: bulk-replace all existing imageUrl http:// prefixes with //.
constraints: Do not change avatar manifest generation process beyond URL scheme

similar_patterns_fixed: none — endpointBaseUrl and manifest imageUrl are the only two avatar URL sources

verification_criteria: avatarManifest.json contains zero occurrences of 'http://wp10665333' in imageUrl fields
generate-avatar-manifest.mjs imageUrl construction uses protocol-relative base
Regression test asserting no http:// imageUrl in manifest PASSES
E2E pre-existing failure (story-04-notes-editor timeout) remains the only E2E failure

failing_e2e_tests: [chromium] › tests/e2e/story-04-notes-editor.spec.ts:34:7 › Story 04 - Notes Editor › user can type notes and see save status (pre-existing timeout failure, unrelated to this bug)
